04 / 11
From prompts to agents

A different kind of tool.

A prompt is a request — one-off, forgotten immediately
vs
An agent is a workflow — it runs, repeats, remembers
A prompt depends on who writes it and how well
vs
An agent delivers consistent output regardless of who uses it
A prompt forgets everything after each session
vs
An agent knows your context, brand and constraints every time

Right now, quality depends on who asked the question. An agent removes that variable.

05 / 11
What makes an agent different

Built for your work.
Shared across your team.

An agent is not a generic tool. It is configured around your specific workflows, your brand guidelines, your data, your constraints.

It knows your tone, your formats, your editorial rules, your priorities.

Once built, anyone on the team can use it — with the same output quality, regardless of AI proficiency.

One agent built once. Used by everyone. Every time.

Examples

Six agents. Already in use.

These are examples. The real list depends on your workflows.

01
📋
Campaign briefing
Structured brief in 2 minutes from plain language
02
✍️
Editorial
Writes, reformulates, adapts tone across formats
03
🔍
Research & synthesis
Benchmark, landscape overview, synthesis note
04
📊
Reporting
Formatted campaign wrap-up from raw data
05
🔎
SEO / GEO assessment
Audits content against search and generative engine criteria
06
💰
Ad budget optimisation
Monitors performance, recommends reallocation

None of these are experimental. Agencies are running them today.

09 / 11
Next steps

Where we go
from here.

NOW

Today you identified the friction. That is the hardest part.

NEXT

We identify together the priority use cases for your team — and on our side, we build the POCs, we test, we iterate.

UNTIL THEN

Note every repetitive task. Every time you think "I have done this before." That is your agent backlog.

Goal

Get the best-fitted agents for the Marketing & Communications Egis community.

10 / 11
References

Sources.

Social Media Examiner (2025) — State of Marketing Report. AI daily usage statistics. 730+ respondents.
Cision (2025) — State of the Media Report. Shadow AI usage, training gaps, legal and privacy concerns.
CoSchedule (2025) — State of AI in Marketing. Job displacement concerns.
Various industry surveys (2024–2025) — AI hallucination concerns among enterprise users.
Penn State University (2024) — Human detection of AI-generated text: 53% accuracy — barely above random chance.
Markowitz et al. (2024) — Linguistic Markers of AI-Generated Communication.
